themes.exampleQuote: A perfect speakeasy vibe with the best cocktails I've had in Pittsburgh.
Alicia was one of the best bartenders I've had, definitely deserves the bar manager position.
A superb riff on a Sazerac/Manhattan/old fashioned. Slightly sweet, little liquorice flavor, booze heavy.
We were informed that we had to leave because there is a 90-minute time limit. This policy is only mentioned in the menu in very small print.
The price reflects the quality, atmosphere and overall experience you're going to have.

notable_quote: I will be making the drive from Cleveland JUST for this bar in the near future.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently praise the moody botanical aesthetic, named bartenders by name, and cocktails built around unusual riffs; the one recurring friction is the 90-minute seating policy, which at least one party felt was enforced without warning.
quarter_narrative: Commerce Bar spent Spring 2026 collecting the kind of reviews that travel, a Cleveland regular promising a return trip, cocktail names like 'absinthe makes the heart grow fonder' written out in full in guest posts, staff singled out by first name. None of that translated into social momentum. One Instagram post in thirty days, zero Reels, and no TikTok presence of any kind means the bar's best stories are staying inside the room. Hidden Harbor, the family leader this issue, built its top rank on social momentum; Commerce is running the opposite playbook right now. Closing the gap to number one means getting the conversation happening online at the same pace it clearly happens at the bar.
tldr_read: Strong Google sentiment and genuinely distinctive cocktail reviews, but near-dormant Instagram and no TikTok footprint keep Commerce Bar at number two in Pittsburgh Bars.
tldr_meaning: The review signal is healthy but plateauing at 106 total; without a social layer, the conversion path and momentum axes have nowhere to grow from here.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Second In Pittsburgh Bars On Review Depth Alone, The Social Signal Has Barely Shown Up.

playbook.headline: Get the bar on Google completely
playbook.action: Add a website URL and phone number to the Google profile; right now both fields are blank, which stalls every potential first visit.

playbook.headline: Post one Reel per week minimum
playbook.action: Commerce Bar posted one Instagram Reel in the last thirty days and zero in the month prior; a weekly cocktail Reel would push engagement past its current 0.72 percent rate.

playbook.headline: Build a TikTok presence from scratch
playbook.action: No Pittsburgh TikTok creator has indexed Commerce Bar yet; a single account posting the cocktail names and the room's candlelit aesthetic could seed that coverage before competitors do.
